title: P1-07: Add OPTIONS preflight depth to CORS check
acceptance_criteria: After completing the existing GET probe loop (which checks origin reflection), add a second pass that sends OPTIONS preflight requests to the same urlsToProbe set (main URL + up to 3 discovered API paths).
Skip the OPTIONS probe for a given URL if the GET probe already emitted a CRITICAL finding for that URL — avoids redundant requests when the worst-case is already found.
OPTIONS request headers: Origin: EVIL_ORIGIN, Access-Control-Request-Method: DELETE, Access-Control-Request-Headers: Authorization. Use the same 8_000ms AbortSignal timeout as the GET probe.

Check the OPTIONS response for Access-Control-Allow-Origin reflection: if the response reflects EVIL_ORIGIN and Access-Control-Allow-Credentials is 'true', emit CRITICAL (same logic as GET probe). If it reflects EVIL_ORIGIN without credentials, emit HIGH.

Additionally, if OPTIONS returns Access-Control-Allow-Methods containing DELETE, PUT, or PATCH on a non-API path (i.e., a path not starting with /api/), emit MEDIUM finding: title='Overpermissive CORS preflight: destructive methods allowed', evidence shows the path and the Allow-Methods value.
Do NOT fail or throw if the OPTIONS request is rejected with a network error or non-2xx status — silently skip that URL.
Test: OPTIONS reflects evil origin with credentials → CRITICAL finding.
Test: OPTIONS reflects evil origin without credentials → HIGH finding.
Test: GET probe already found CRITICAL → OPTIONS probe skipped for that URL (verify no extra fetch call).
Test: OPTIONS response includes DELETE in Access-Control-Allow-Methods on non-API path → MEDIUM finding.
Test: OPTIONS request throws network error → no finding, no exception.
All existing P1-07 GET-probe tests still pass.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
